sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.

sections.instruction: Questions 11-16: Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G next to questions 11-16. Questions 17-18: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 19-20: Choose TWO letters A-E.

options: A not dependent on season
B enjoyable
C low risk of injury
D fitness level unimportant
E sociable
F fast results
G motivating

questions.content: For which TWO reasons does the speaker say people give up going to the gym?
options: A lack of time
B loss of confidence
C too much effort required
D high costs
E feeling less successful than others

questions.content: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give for setting goals?
options: A write goals down
B have achievable aims
C set a time limit
D give yourself rewards
E challenge yourself

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 25-30: Choose SIX answers from the options below and write correct letter A-H next to questions 25-30.
sections.passage_or_context: Project on using natural dyes to colour fabrics

questions.content: What first inspired Jim to choose this project?
options: A textiles displayed in an exhibition
B a book about a botanic garden
C carpets he saw on holiday

questions.content: Jim eventually decided to do a practical investigation which involved
options: A using a range of dyes with different fibres
B applying different dyes to one type of fibre
C testing one dye and a range of fibres

questions.content: When doing his experiments Jim was surprised by
options: A how much natural material was needed to make the dye
B the fact that dyes were widely available on the internet
C the time that he had to leave the fabric in the dye

questions.content: What problem did Jim have with using tartrazine as a fabric dye?
options: A it caused a slight allergic reaction
B it was not a permanent dye on cotton
C it was ineffective when used on nylon
A it is expensive
B the color is too strong
C the color is not long lasting
D it is very poisonous
E it can damage the fabric
F the color may be unexpected
G it is unsuitable for some fabrics
H it is not generally available

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
The sleepy lizard (tiliqua rugose)
Description
• They are common in western and south Australia
• They are brown but recognisable by their blue (31) {{BLANK}}
• They are relatively large
• Their diet consists mainly of (32) {{BLANK}}
• Their main predators are large birds and (33) {{BLANK}}
Navigation study
• One study found that lizards can use the (34) {{BLANK}} to help them navigate
Observations in the wild
• Observations show that these lizards keep the same (35) {{BLANK}} for several years
What people want
• Possible reasons:
o To improve the survival of their young (but little (36) {{BLANK}} has been noted between parents and children)
o To provide (37) {{BLANK}} for female lizards
Tracking study
• A study was carried out using GPS systems attached to the (38) {{BLANK}} of the lizards
• This provided information on the lizards’ location and even the number of (39) {{BLANK}} taken
• It appeared that the lizards were trying to avoid one another
• This may be in order to reduce chances of (40) {{BLANK}}
